title: OPTIONS COUNSELING - 211info
text: WASHINGTON COUNTY DISABILITY, AGING AND VETERAN SERVICES OPTIONS COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Options counselors help individuals make informed long-term care decisions. Counselors help individuals determine their strengths, needs, preferences, and unique situations. Provides information about support strategies, action plans, and tactics based on the choices available in the community. Options Counseling is a service for individuals to plan for their current or future care needs and maintain independence. Options counselors assist in problem-solving, advocacy, and connecting participants to resources and community support. 5240 NE Elam Young Parkway Suite 300 Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and their family members or caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-3060 4 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 846-3060 ext 4 Toll-free (855) 673-2372 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Washington County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Near Hawthorn Farm MAX station Services: Case/Care Management * Older Adults Case/Care Management * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Long Term Care Options Counseling Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE HOUSING/SHELTER - CARE FACILITIES AND SENIOR HOUSING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ADULT PROTECTIVE SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BENEFITS ENROLLMENT CENTER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID MONEY MANAGEMENT - MONEY MANAGEMENT PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM VOLUNTEER OPPORTUNITIES - VOLUNTEERS PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
